audit_domains.name: test-authenticity
audit_domains.rationale: RigorFoundry's conclusions depend on tests exercising real Git, filesystem, process, packaging, and CLI boundaries.

audit_domains.name: architecture-and-wiring
audit_domains.rationale: The scanner, evidence models, campaign protocol, CLI, and repository guards have explicit ownership and dependency boundaries.

audit_domains.name: godfile-responsibility
audit_domains.rationale: Large mixed-responsibility scanner or protocol modules would make audit conclusions harder to review and maintain.

audit_domains.name: application-security
audit_domains.rationale: RigorFoundry parses untrusted repositories and invokes explicitly configured subprocesses, so static security analysis is mandatory.

audit_domains.name: supply-chain
audit_domains.rationale: Build, CI, test, documentation, and security environments are hash locked and require vulnerability review.

audit_domains.name: api-abi-schema-compatibility
audit_domains.rationale: Versioned policies, reports, reviews, campaigns, attestations, and comparisons are public machine-readable contracts.

audit_domains.name: scientific-numerical-correctness
audit_domains.rationale: The core orchestrates repository evidence and contains no scientific model, numerical solver, statistical estimator, or floating-point acceptance claim.

audit_domains.name: reliability-and-concurrency
audit_domains.rationale: Audit evidence is immutable, content addressed, path confined, and written under create-only and lock-aware storage contracts.

audit_domains.name: performance-and-reproducibility
audit_domains.rationale: Repeated scans over identical tracked inputs must be deterministic and native adapters are bounded by explicit timeouts.

audit_domains.name: data-and-privacy
audit_domains.rationale: Repositories may contain sensitive material; the dependency-free core is local-only and must not acquire an undeclared network client.

audit_domains.name: operations-and-observability
audit_domains.rationale: CLI exit codes, bounded diagnostics, report digests, and campaign attestations are operational interfaces.

audit_domains.name: packaging-deployment-iac
audit_domains.rationale: The project ships a Python distribution, a non-root container, documentation, releases, and immutable automation.

audit_domains.name: documentation-claims-ip
audit_domains.rationale: Public claims, citation metadata, Apache-2.0 licensing, copyright, action pins, and release metadata must agree.

audit_domains.name: ownership-and-maintenance
audit_domains.rationale: Module-named test owners, CODEOWNERS, contribution policy, and independent review are mandatory maintenance evidence.
